what_it_does: A curated harvest ledger for the projection session that records the build status of the FACE-1 operator surface and Right-Hand-Man assistant layers, tagging each claim with a verification state, listing relations, open questions, dead-ends, and provenance.
observations: Every claim carries a verification tag such as verified, attempted-unverified, broken, or abandoned plus a reason.
The ledger is rooted at board://item-ef7eb599 and links 13 typed-edge board records.
It explicitly states that the operator loop never closed on a real Tim decision end-to-end.
It records five measurement false-negatives and a falsely logged verified channel-view bug.
L1 grounded-explain streams on the decision card are verified only for the theorem-fork subtype.
L2 ask is partially built but routes to a generic endpoint rather than the grounded explain role.
L4 channel-post composer is form-verified but never shown to land a real post due to a two-store split.
L5 propose/accept is route-built but end-to-end unverified and held for a Tim walk-through.
Timeline and address surfaces are blocked on unshipped DNA shapes.
The file refuses to inherit ~1800 prior verified lines from the loop doc.
standouts: The headline honesty gap: the real Tim operator loop was never demonstrated to close.
The look beats the inference is elevated from a debugging lesson to an architectural law.
A previously logged verified channel-view was actually a 162px rendering island.
L4 posts dead-lettered because the cc_channel store differs from the bridge's fold_channels store.
L5 is held not just technically but by a new user-facing-walks-through-Tim rule.
conventions: Uses a strict verification taxonomy and demands re-checking in the current session rather than inheriting green status.
References artifacts with board://item-... IDs and commit hashes.
Structures content as WHAT I AM, CLAIMS, NOT-DONE, RELATIONS, OPEN QUESTIONS, DEAD-ENDS, and PROVENANCE.
Labels typed edges such as derives_from, composes_with, blocked_by, refutes, and same_law.
Breaks the convention of trusting committed code or prior records by requiring live service verification.
concerns: A large portion of the claimed surface remains attempted-unverified or held.
The operator-loop gap undermines the stated purpose of enabling Tim to decide and execute through the surface.
Several earlier verified states were not re-confirmed this session.
Heavy reliance on unshipped DNA shapes leaves timeline and address surfaces unresolved.
The verification discipline is itself vulnerable to the same measurement contamination it warns about.
notes: A self-audited build ledger that treats its own prior verified records as suspect and puts the unclosed real-user operator loop at the top of the carry-forward list.
questions: What are DNA, the bridge, the fork, and recollection in this context?
What do board://item-... identifiers resolve to and where are the 13 board records stored?
What is the V and what distinguishes FACE-1 from other faces?
What does verify-mode do beyond suppressing decision_take writes?
Which exact endpoint or store is the live path for L4 posts?
What is the new user-facing-walks-through-Tim rule that gates L5?
purpose_vs_actual: It matches in intent: the file is a harvest/retrospective ledger rather than a build plan, and it documents both what was built and the honesty gap around real operator closure.
apparent_themes: Epistemic humility and distrust of green claims and green measurements.
Verification-by-use in a trusted context rather than by record or inference.
Server-decided, from-DNA architectural laws over client flags or bespoke fixes.
Honest degradation and explicit blocker documentation.
Operator-loop closure as the ultimate acceptance criterion.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create an honest, resumable checkpoint that resists self-certification, captures why things remain unverified, and encodes the lesson that observing the actual behavior beats trusting measurements or records.
novelty: It is unusual for a build ledger to explicitly invalidate its own prior verified entries, use a verification taxonomy tied to board records, and treat the failure to close a real-user loop as the primary deliverable rather than a hidden risk.
contribution: A tagged, evidence-linked status map of the FACE-1/RHM build that preserves verification boundaries, blocker taxonomy, measurement-contamination lessons, and the unresolved operator-loop gap for anyone resuming the work.
summary_for_embedding: A projection harvest ledger documenting the build status, verification claims, blockers, and unresolved real-user operator-loop gap for the FACE-1 operator surface and Right-Hand-Man assistant layers.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a self-audited, evidence-tagged account of what was built, what remains unverified, and what must happen next to close the real operator loop.
